displayTitle: Wet the Surface, Give Capillarity Time

beats.narration: Application looks easy — point the can and spray. But this is the stage where capillary action either gets what it needs, or it doesn't.

payload.title: APPLICATION & DWELL
payload.sub: Wet film on, kept wet, long enough.

beats.narration: Three workhorse ways to get penetrant on: spray for local welds and production, brush for a precise small zone, immersion for baskets of parts.

tiles.s: One seam, dye where you want

beats.narration: Whatever the method, you need one thing: a continuous wet film over every surface, plus a margin. Then capillary action slowly draws penetrant into the flaw.

payload.heading: Why dwell takes time
payload.note: WET FILM → CAPILLARY ENTRY → FLAW FILLED AT ~10 MIN

beats.narration: Stay in the band, roughly forty to one twenty-five Fahrenheit. Cold penetrant is slow, so extend dwell. Too hot and the film dries.
So the rhythm is steady. Wet every surface, start the clock, and keep the film wet until the procedure says time. Give capillarity what it needs.

payload.sub: A dried film stops feeding the flaw — reprocess from the start.
